extraction.one_line_summary: DEGENKOLB John won from a three-man breakaway that escaped at 17km, holding off chase group led by Jungels with Sagan and Gilbert.
tactical_patterns: STUYVEN Jasper's early attack pulled DEGENKOLB's chase group clear at 17km, classic teammate sacrifice on cobbles.
BMC positioned VAN AVERMAET perfectly by having teammates wait mid-race to pace him back after crashes.
MOVISTAR surprisingly strong on cobbles with QUINTANA marking FROOME's late attack, defying pre-race expectations.
Multiple mechanicals devastated BARDET's team depth—eight-man rosters left no recovery firepower after third cobble sector.

rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: positive: First win since career-threatening 2016 accident, proves he's regained elite cobbles form
rider_signals.FULGSANG Jakob: positive: Strongest GC rider on cobbles, marked as unexpected threat for future bad-weather/technical stages
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Lost 1:28 after multiple flats starting at third cobble sector, team exhausted chasing repeatedly
rider_signals.URAN Rigoberto: negative: Lost nearly 3 minutes with slow bike change during breakaway formation, poor team positioning
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Movistar defied expectations by riding cobbles confidently, even marked Froome's late attack
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Crashed over teammate Moscon's bike but recovered instantly, attacked late despite incident
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: Kept yellow despite slowing for Froome crash, showed restraint and tactical maturity
